# Transcrição: 02 ADVANCED MATCHING

- **Fonte:** 02 ADVANCED MATCHING
- **Duração:** 06:30
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:02]

So in this video, we're going to go over advanced matching in the Facebook pixel, what it is, and how to set it up. So advanced matching lets you take advantage of your own customer data, like email addresses and phone numbers and so on, so that you can report on or collect data on more of the conversions that are occurring on your website. So this then enables you to optimize your ads based on a higher amount of data, and reach more people and custom audiences or in DPAs. So you can pass on customer identifiers like emails or phone numbers, which you'll collect at your checkout or registration or sign-in events on your website, and you pass those straight on to Facebook. So Facebook then takes that information and it hashes the data so that the privacy is still protected, and it matches that data to pixel events where the Facebook cookie isn't present on the browser. So if someone isn't logged in to Facebook or if they're browsing in incognito mode, the pixel is still tracking and it's still registering those events and conversions, but it's not tracking it to a particular user. So Facebook can't match up who that person is, and therefore they can't put them in custom audiences, or give you the full capabilities of your DPA campaigns, for example. So in order to set up advanced matching, what you need to do is edit the base pixel code to include the advanced matching parameters that you want to track. So you may have noticed here when you go to set up your pixel using the HTML method, that you'll see this button at the bottom saying use advanced matching, and if you toggle that on, you'll see it's a very small change in the code, but it adds in this EM insert email variable. So turn that off, it'll disappear again. So in there, you can also track other parameters like phone numbers, first name, last name. There's actually a whole list of parameters that you can track with Facebook here, so this is just on the developer's site, I'll pop a link to this underneath this video so you guys can see which parameters you can track and what you need to include in the code so that Facebook can register what it is. So what you would do, I'll just pull up the text editor I have open. So this is essentially the standard Facebook pixel code, and you can see this bit in here in red that I've highlighted. This is the bit that's going to be edited to include the advanced matching parameters. So this is it standard with just the pixel code in there, and then the bits that you added in are, I'll just copy this over. So you can see this is the bit that I had highlighted in red. And now it's included EM insert email variable.

## [03:02 - 06:08]

So this bit here is what you need to change depending on your website, depending on what platform you're using. So you don't want to leave this in as insert email variable because then you'll get an error on your pixel helper saying that Facebook couldn't match up the email variable because this is not your email variable. So in here you'll pull up depending on what your website is. So this is an example of the email of all of the generic customer variables on WooCommerce. So if we wanted to track someone's email, we'd come down here and pull this customer email variable. And that would go in there. So what this is essentially doing is telling Facebook what to look for when pulling your customer email addresses. So this is you telling Facebook. So Facebook recognizes this EM as meaning email. Then that's the variable that Facebook is looking for. And this bit over here is you telling Facebook, okay, an email address on my website is called customer underscore email. So then Facebook can look through and find this customer underscore email in your website or in the contact form that's pulled through or the checkout data. And it knows that the data that's associated with this variable is going to be that person's email address. So let's say for this one, we also want to track first name, last name and phone number. So to separate the different advanced matching parameters that you're going to be tracking, you need to put in a comma as well. So then we'll just copy and paste this. And now instead of email, we're going to track first name. So you'll find all of your variables that you can track in here. So first name is FN. So we'll change that to FN. And then our WooCommerce variable for that is customer underscore first underscore name. And we'll pop that in there. And then we'll do the same for last name, which is LM. And then instead of customer first name, that's just customer last name. And then we want phone number. So the phone number variable is just pH. And then our WooCommerce variable. There we go. So for the phone number, for example, on WooCommerce, you can either pull through the billing phone number or the shipping phone number. So let's just pull through the billing number in there. Cool. So then what we would do is if the pixel is already installed, you're going to replace what you had here just with the pixel and the code with this whole section.

## [06:08 - 06:30]

Otherwise, if this is starting from scratch and you're installing a brand new pixel onto a website, then you've now got your full code ready to go and you can just pop all of that into the header for your website. And that's going to track all that extra data and pass that onto Facebook so that you can expand your audiences and track more conversion data.
